stimulus: The extreme cold encountered in polar lakes can damage the membranes of microbes living in the water, disrupting normal cell function. A class of molecules called antifreeze glycoproteins (AFGPs) prevents this damage by binding to small ice crystals and stopping them from ______ are produced in unusually large amounts in the cells of polar-adapted bacteria.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
rationale:
Choice D is correct. A period correctly marks the boundary between the two independent clauses ("A class...growing" and "AFGPs...bacteria").

Choice A creates a run-on with no punctuation. Choice B uses "and" without a comma to join two independent clauses, creating a comma splice when paired with no preceding comma — improper conjunction use here. Choice C creates a comma splice.
